questions.scenario: A tiny tag-only pull request produces a plan that wants to replace the production database. What is the right review response?

options.text: Stop and investigate why the evidence conflicts with the story.
Approve because any plan output is safe.
Ignore the plan because tags are boring.

questions.explanation: Run evidence should be compared with the intended change before approval.

questions.scenario: An Ansible run reports `changed` on a service restart every time, even when the config did not change. What might be wrong?

options.text: The task may be written in a way that is not idempotent.
Services cannot be managed by Ansible.
No reviewer should read run output.

questions.explanation: Repeated unnecessary changes are a sign to inspect task design and handler behavior.

questions.scenario: Which run evidence should make reviewers slow down?

options.text: A failed run that may have left partial changes.
No changes when no changes were expected.

questions.explanation: Destroy, replacement, and partial failure can carry real operational risk.
